title: Why he’s running the Boston Marathon in memory of family treated at Dana-Farber
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Trevor Murray, 30, from Malden, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of family treated at Dana-Farber.
Murray's father, Jeff, and sister-in-law, Grace, were treated at Dana-Farber and passed away in 2020.
Training for his first marathon, Murray is inspired by his father who ran during cancer treatment in 2018.

title: MBTA announces schedule changes, detours as nearly 1 million expected to attend Sunday’s St. Patrick’s Day parade
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: MBTA announces schedule changes for St. Patrick's Day parade, expecting nearly 1 million attendees in South Boston.
Red Line to operate on rush hour schedule; possible Broadway stop bypass due to heavy parade crowds.
Shuttle buses and extra train cars added; bus routes 9 and 11 suspended, others detoured on parade day.

title: Boston Public Schools graduation rate reached all-time high last year, still lags behind state average
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston Public Schools' graduation rate reached a record 81.3% in 2025, up 1.6% from 2024.
The district's graduation rate remains 8 percentage points below the Massachusetts state average of 89.3%.
Notable increases were seen among English learners and students with disabilities, each rising over 5%.

title: Bruins’ playoff hopes are destined to fail if they can’t solve confounding road woes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins are struggling on the road with an 11-14-5 record despite their playoff position.
Boston's GM, Don Sweeney, expressed concern about the team's inability to win away games, crucial for playoff success.
With 12 of their final 21 games on the road, the Bruins' playoff prospects are uncertain if they don't improve away performance.

title: This Marblehead man is running the Boston Marathon for his pediatric patients
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brian Wishart, 42, from Marblehead, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for his pediatric patients.
Wishart is Chief of Pediatric Rehabilitation Medicine at Spaulding Rehab, supporting hospital and adaptive sports programs.
He aims to integrate Spaulding's research into clinical care, ensuring all children receive cutting-edge treatments.

title: 8 children have now died from flu in Mass. this season
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Eight children and 298 adults have died from flu in Massachusetts this season as of February 28.
February flu data showed low severity in Boston, with 38.1% of residents vaccinated this season.
Health officials continue to recommend flu vaccination for everyone six months and older despite nearing end of flu season.

title: NBA cancels Hawks’ ‘Magic City’ promotion amid backlash following Luke Kornet’s letter
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: NBA cancels Atlanta Hawks' 'Magic City Monday' promotion after concerns from stakeholders, including fans and partners.
The promotion, scheduled for March 16, was to feature Magic City's chicken wings and a performance by T.I.
Former Celtics center Luke Kornet's viral letter questioned the appropriateness of the promotion for a family-friendly atmosphere.

title: Shaquille O’Neal had no clue who Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman is
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Shaquille O'Neal was unaware of Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman until seeing highlights against the Cavaliers.
Baylor Scheierman, a first-round pick in 2024, has played 59 games for the Celtics this season, starting in 16.
Despite a thumb fracture, Scheierman is averaging 4.8 points, 3.2 rebounds, and 1.2 assists per game this season.

title: Healey calls on aviation company operating at Hanscom to cut ties with ICE
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey urges Signature Aviation to cease assisting ICE deportation flights at Hanscom Field, Bedford.
Signature Aviation is identified as essential in coordinating deportation operations for ICE at Hanscom Field, handling 132 flights last year.
Healey criticizes ICE's deportation practices, asserting they violate legal rights and disproportionately target non-criminal immigrants.

title: Delivery person falls through a shaft outside Boston’s Omni Parker House
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A delivery worker was injured after falling 20 feet through a metal grate at Omni Parker House in Boston.
The Boston Inspectional Services is issuing a building code violation due to the grate failure caused by rotted brackets.
The hotel must hire a structural engineer to inspect all vent grates for integrity and submit a repair report.
